questions.question: What is the primary difference between the Glob tool and the Grep tool?
options: Glob is faster than Grep
Glob matches file names/paths; Grep searches file contents
Glob works recursively; Grep only searches one directory
Glob supports regex; Grep only supports literal strings
questions.explanation: Glob finds files by name patterns (e.g., **/*.js), while Grep searches inside files for content matching a regex pattern. They serve complementary purposes.

questions.question: What is the default output_mode of the Grep tool?
options: content
count
files_with_matches
verbose
questions.explanation: The default output_mode is 'files_with_matches', which returns only the file paths containing matches. Use 'content' mode to see the actual matching lines.

questions.question: When should you enable the multiline flag in the Grep tool?
options: When searching for patterns longer than 80 characters
When searching in files with more than 1000 lines
When the pattern needs to match across multiple lines
When using the count output mode
questions.explanation: Multiline mode (multiline: true) allows patterns to span multiple lines, with '.' matching newlines. By default, patterns only match within single lines.

questions.question: How are Glob results sorted?
options: Alphabetically by file path
By file size (largest first)
By modification time (most recent first)
By directory depth (shallowest first)
questions.explanation: Glob results are sorted by modification time with the most recently modified files first. This is useful for finding actively worked-on files.

questions.question: When is the Agent tool with subagent_type=Explore the best choice for searching?
options: When you know exactly which file to search
When you need a simple regex search across the codebase
When the search is open-ended and may require multiple rounds of exploration
When you need to search only file names
questions.explanation: The Agent/Explore tool is best for open-ended research questions that require multiple iterative rounds of searching and reading, like 'How does authentication work in this codebase?'

questions.question: Why should you NEVER use bash grep or rg commands directly?
options: They are slower than the Grep tool
They don't support regex
The Grep tool has been optimized for correct permissions and access
Bash commands can't search recursively
questions.explanation: The Grep tool has been specifically optimized for correct permissions and access within the Claude Code environment. Using bash grep or rg bypasses these optimizations.
